assertions.description: HTMLCanvasElement is exposed because WebGL context creation starts from canvas.getContext().
HTMLCanvasElement.prototype.getContext is exposed and is the API that must now return a real context or null.
WebGLRenderingContext remains defined; Chrome 139 changes the renderer fallback path, not the WebGL API surface.
getContext('webgl', { failIfMajorPerformanceCaveat: true }) returns either a WebGL context without known software-renderer tokens or null.
assertions.test: (()=>{try{const c=document.createElement('canvas');const ctx=c.getContext('webgl',{failIfMajorPerformanceCaveat:true});if(ctx===null)return true;if(!(ctx instanceof WebGLRenderingContext))return false;const debug=ctx.getExtension('WEBGL_debug_renderer_info');const renderer=debug?ctx.getParameter(debug.UNMASKED_RENDERER_WEBGL):ctx.getParameter(ctx.RENDERER);return !/swiftshader|llvmpipe|softpipe|software rasterizer|microsoft basic render|gdi generic|mesa offscreen/i.test(String(renderer));}catch(e){return false;}})()

assertions.description: When WebGL2 is exposed, getContext('webgl2', { failIfMajorPerformanceCaveat: true }) also returns either a hardware-like context or null.
assertions.test: (()=>{try{if(!('WebGL2RenderingContext' in globalThis))return true;const c=document.createElement('canvas');const ctx=c.getContext('webgl2',{failIfMajorPerformanceCaveat:true});if(ctx===null)return true;if(!(ctx instanceof WebGL2RenderingContext))return false;const debug=ctx.getExtension('WEBGL_debug_renderer_info');const renderer=debug?ctx.getParameter(debug.UNMASKED_RENDERER_WEBGL):ctx.getParameter(ctx.RENDERER);return !/swiftshader|llvmpipe|softpipe|software rasterizer|microsoft basic render|gdi generic|mesa offscreen/i.test(String(renderer));}catch(e){return false;}})()

assertions.description: WebGLContextEvent is exposed so pages can observe context creation errors, loss, and restoration instead of leaving a blank canvas.
If WEBGL_lose_context is exposed, it provides loseContext() and restoreContext() for exercising the same loss branch the demo handles.
CanvasRenderingContext2D is available as a practical fallback renderer when WebGL context creation returns null.
A page can create a separate Canvas 2D fallback after probing WebGL, avoiding reuse of a canvas that already owns a WebGL context.
